title: Parking Attendants
description: Park vehicles or issue tickets for customers in a parking lot or garage. May park or tend vehicles in environments such as a car dealership or rental car facility. May collect fee.

tasks.text: Take numbered tags from customers, locate vehicles, and deliver vehicles, or provide customers with instructions for locating vehicles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect vehicles to detect any damage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Greet customers and open their car doors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Issue ticket stubs or place numbered tags on windshields, log tags or attach tag to customers' keys, and give customers matching tags for locating parked vehicles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform cash handling tasks, such as making change, balancing and recording cash drawer, or distributing tips.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain and calculate parking charges, collect fees from customers, and respond to customer complaints.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Park and retrieve automobiles for customers in parking lots, storage garages, or new car lots.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide customer assistance and information, such as giving directions or handling wheelchairs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep parking areas clean and orderly to ensure that space usage is maximized.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Call emergency responders or the proper authorities and provide motorist assistance, such as giving directions or helping jump start a stalled vehicle.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Patrol parking areas to prevent vehicle damage and vehicle or property thefts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct motorists to parking areas or parking spaces, using hand signals or flashlights as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Escort customers to their vehicles to ensure their safety.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform maintenance on cars in storage to protect tires, batteries, or exteriors from deterioration.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Lift, position, and remove barricades to open or close parking areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
